_notes: Same shape as exa_search_response.json but produced when category='research paper' is sent in the POST body. URLs are academic sources (arxiv, pubmed, nature, etc). score is optional and may be absent on some entries.

results.title: Language Models are Few-Shot Learners (GPT-3)
results.author: Tom B. Brown et al.
results.text: We demonstrate that scaling language models greatly improves task-agnostic, few-shot performance, sometimes even reaching competitiveness with prior state-of-the-art fine-tuning approaches. GPT-3 has 175 billion parameters and achieves strong performance on many NLP benchmarks.
highlights: scaling language models greatly improves task-agnostic, few-shot performance

results.title: Highly accurate protein structure prediction with AlphaFold
results.author: John Jumper et al.
results.text: Proteins are essential to life and understanding their structure would unlock progress in drug discovery. We present AlphaFold, a novel machine learning approach that achieves atomic accuracy in protein structure prediction.
highlights: novel machine learning approach that achieves atomic accuracy in protein structure prediction

results.title: LLaMA: Open and Efficient Foundation Language Models
results.author: Hugo Touvron et al.
results.text: We introduce LLaMA, a collection of foundation language models ranging from 7B to 65B parameters. We train these models on trillions of tokens and show that it is possible to train state-of-the-art models using publicly available datasets exclusively.
highlights: foundation language models ranging from 7B to 65B parameters

results.title: Transformer models for medical image segmentation
results.author: Various
results.text: Medical image segmentation using transformer-based architectures has demonstrated superior performance over convolutional approaches for 3D volumetric data.
